title: Gaffrey Lab Infant Microstates and Attention
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 43 infants aged 5-10 months during a passive viewing task involving relaxing videos with sound. The study investigates infant attention and microstate dynamics as part of a larger investigation into the effects of bias and discrimination on maternal health and infant neurodevelopment. EEG was recorded using a 128-channel geodesic sensor net while infants watched video stimuli presented with attention-grabbing cues.
methods_description: EEG was recorded at 1000 Hz using a 128-channel HydroCel Geodesic Sensor Net referenced to vertex (Cz), with impedances maintained below 50 kΩ. Infants (N=43, aged 5-10 months) sat on caregiver's lap and viewed up to 15 minutes of relaxing videos (ten 90-second videos with breaks). Three-second attention-grabber videos preceded each stimulus presentation. Data collection was conducted in accordance with Duke University Health System IRB approval and the Declaration of Helsinki.
